metadata.title: Why a missing math prerequisite can't be scaffolded around — only routed through
metadata.topic: Math knowledge compounding and the prerequisite-gap mechanism
metadata.purpose: Math compounds — each concept sits directly on a prior one. A missing prerequisite leaves the grade-level scaffold floating. The below-tier scaffold must route through the prerequisite first, not simplify the grade-level task.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.still_lanes: geo (tower with missing block), c2v (fraction → ratio bridge)
metadata.exclusions: the CCSS fallback chain, individual student diagnosis, algebra
metadata.visual_object: A vertical tower of concept blocks; one prerequisite block is missing, so the grade-level block above hangs unsupported.
metadata.hook: In ELA and science, scaffolding grade-level content works for below-level students. In math, if the prerequisite concept is missing, no scaffold for the grade-level concept will catch.

beats.narration_text: A 6th grader meets ratio reasoning. The teacher adds double number lines, ratio tables, sentence supports. Nothing moves. The real gap is 5th-grade fraction equivalence — the scaffolds have nothing to attach to.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: Why does a well-designed grade-level scaffold sometimes fail completely — and what makes math structurally different from ELA here?
In ELA and science, scaffolding grade-level content works for below-level students. In math, if the prerequisite concept is missing, no scaffold for the grade-level concept will catch.

Math compounds — each concept sits directly on a prior one. A missing prerequisite leaves the grade-level scaffold floating. The below-tier scaffold must route through the prerequisite first, not simplify the grade-level task.
beats.visual: A vertical tower of concept blocks; one prerequisite block is missing, so the grade-level block above hangs unsupported.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: 5th grade: 2/4 = 1/2. 6th grade: 2:4 = 1:2. A below-level 6th grader without fraction equivalence can't absorb ratio scaffolds; the scaffold must first reactivate fraction equivalence via a tape diagram, then bridge to ratio notation.

Math compounds — each concept sits directly on a prior one. A missing prerequisite leaves the grade-level scaffold floating. The below-tier scaffold must route through the prerequisite first, not simplify the grade-level task.
